Off Calendar Sep-07-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Katherine L. Alphonso, Esq., KAUFMAN DOLOWICH & VOLUCK, LLP, (415) 926-7600, for Cross Defendant JERICHO PROJECT (erroneously sued as JERICHO CONSTRUCTION)
Joel P. Glaser, Esq., SKANE MILLS LLP, (415) 431-4150, jglaser@skanemills.com, for Defendant T. B. PENICK & SONS, INC.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT JERICHO PROJECT DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT

Jericho Project's demurrer to the cross-complaint is sustained with leave to amend.

Jericho was brought into this case as Roe 2. The cross-complaint is unclear as to what the allegations against Jericho/Roe 2 are. The fourth cause of action is brought "Against All Cross-Defendants and ROES 1 through 140," while the other three counts are only "Against All Cross-Defendants." It is unclear what "written contract[s]" by Jericho is/are alleged. (See X-Cmplt. 6:24.) It is also unclear what fault by Jericho is alleged. In short, the cross-complaint does not meet the pleading requirements of CCP 425.10.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
